'''Venans''' were pale-skinned [[Human/Legends|Humans]] from [[Vena]].

==History==
Descended from early [[Hapan]] [[Scout/Legends|explorers]], the Venans had a tradition of maintaining an independent and isolationist stance on their system, remaining unaffiliated from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], even though they shared a good relationship and the Venans would occasionally trade with the nearby [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]].<ref name="btr">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20020411crise&c=rpg|text=Before the Rise}}</ref>

Circa [[57 BBY/Legends|57 BBY]], the rise of [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Kindoro]] and Baroness [[Omnino]] to power on [[Vena]] changed this historic position. Due to the fact that Kindoro and Omnino had been both [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadors]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], this contact with outsiders motivated an economic deregulation and active foreign policy, including trade agreements, in order to make goods accessible to the Venan royal family.<ref name="btr" />

This radical shift angered many Venans, who resented the fact that only the royal family was benefitting from the economic opening. The commoners called for a return to isolationism and the old-fashioned values. The regents, unwilling to give in to popular demands, imprisoned the [[Vena Isolationists|Vena isolationism]] movement leaders and sent them to labor camps. This move aggravated tensions and motivated the emergence of terrorism and sabotage against Venan nobility.<ref name="btr" />

This conflict was worsened with the death of Kindoro, as Baroness Omnino proved herself even more unmerciful than her husband, and waged [[Venan civil war|bloody war]] for years against her subjects,<ref name="btr" /> until her death in [[37 BBY/Legends|37 BBY]].<ref name="Last3" />
